item.title: Re: Albumin
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I have done now.

item.title: Re: Albumin
item.content:encoded: I think you're correct about the spelling—but I recommend messaging the original author directly to be certain.

item.title: Albumin
item.content:encoded: While albumin is a real thing (a family of proteins), in the context of this SCP presumably you mean albumen (i.e. egg white)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a beautiful baby boy who just loves wailin' when he's too cranky to sleep, and you know what? Every time he does, I think about that scream. I see it in my head. Think what it did- and his pa gives him a look as if he's gonna bash his fuckin' head against the wall.

fuuuuuuuck

+1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: proof that I am weird: my first thought upon reading that it's half chicken and half chimp was to eat one, since it should taste like half poultry half pork (I'd assume that chimp meat tastes like human meat, which can, allegedly, be convincingly passed off as pork).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder, is the brown hardening substance mentionned linked to similar substance in SCP-093, in the alternate reality of the Red Sea Object ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read SCPs sequentially until I found this, so I could ask:

"Why did the SCP-3199 cross the road?"

"because it's unoriginal, but still good"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh god nooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo

no

no no no no no

+1

no no no

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That part where Cpl Duncan talks about his son reminding him of the chicken death scream…fuck. There's a lot I enjoy here, but that moment has really stuck with me

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Contaiment Procedure: VITRIFY IT.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i've made it this far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any thoughts on the dark brown watery paste that turns hard when exposed to air?
A deep fried coating joke?
I didn't get that.
Also, is the origin of this supposed to have been a mad person that plucked chickens with his teeth and then cut them up and sewed those parts to his or another person's body? The chimp DNA is either because they are so close to out DNA that the traces were misunderstood or it's an actual chicken-monkey?
It's all quite mad and very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn’t that picture violate the rules? It’s got nudity, the thing has… man bits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"didnt you want this?"

"Protocol 34-22-B - 'Poached'"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Terrifyingly disgusting to the extent that the anomalies aren't easily containable let alone a disgusting result of a triangle of love between mutliple animals and a human.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At least you dont become a chicken thing
Except the first guy
Congrats to our precious chicken bois

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) The containment procedures say that all live instances are to be in primary containment and that all live instances are to be in secondary containment. Is one supposed to be for live hatched instance and the other live unhatched instances?

2) Do they produce the matter for new eggs out of nothing? If so, you should make the more explicit, and also contact the tag team to have them add ectoentropic to the tag list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So have an automated turret do it. Or else chuck them in a vat of acid, as the liquid will prevent them from laying more and kill any that do hatch. Or just encase them all in concrete and bury them under the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But then THAT baby has another egg inside it.
Infinite loop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is it so hard to contain? Just wait for the egg to hatch and shoot the baby immediately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"haha it has a peepee" - George Washington

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Classic, yet original monster SCiP. Very evocative of Series I stuff in the best kind of way. Easy +1. Not everything has to be full of overarching narrative, plot twists, and recurring PoIs/GoIs. The mainlist needs some good ol' creature horror every now and again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know why. This SCP gave me series 1 vibes.
Good work (And terrifying picture)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that it works fine, it's just that I don't fully understand the origin story. Was it a scientific experiment gone wrong? A supernatural being granting someone's wish for eternal life? Arts and crafts gone wrong? I need info!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article was sitting at +319 when I finished reading it. The resistance to upvote was unbearable…

But alas, my craving for human-chicken hybrid gave in.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair, that's what worked for me — like, the diary is so over-the-top that it comes off as more pathetic than genuinely creepy. Like a moody teenager trying to be edgy.

Which actually makes it way creepier for me, because moody teenagers summoning eldritch chicken-apes to refute the existence of humanity is kind of terrifying (and you know that if it was possible, moody teenagers would totally do it).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I'm going to complain about this a lot in discussion threads but I don't like the diary much. The creature looks scary and what it does is scary, but the diary tries way too hard to be creepy (a lot of people have this problem and I'm probably one of them). I've got a couple suggestions.

+ show block
– hide block

I'm assuming the title "Humans, Refuted" is a reference to the time that Diogenes the Cynic trolled Plato by refuting his assertion that humans are unique as "featherless bipeds" by showing him a plucked chicken.

I feel like referencing that - or straight-up quoting it - would be a way more effective method of conveying what the guy seems to be doing, which is creating an animal that would replace humans as the dominant species on the planet + be a parody of them. Correct me if I'm wrong here, of course; I wouldn't want to trample over your work.

So you get your spooky sheets of paper and the final one just says "Behold…A MAN!" and it wraps everything up in a more effective bow than standard "I'm trying to sound like I'm mentally ill"-speak.

+1 though.
I’m at a loss for words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me reading this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've convinced me to upvote and also to stop wondering why this is Keter. Now I'm just wondering why bother with a humanoid containment room when they're all cast into blocks of acrylic anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God this SCP is so sexy. Like damn, that's one thicc chicken man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just imagine being the guy taking the picture of the SCP-3199…

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Staff only deletes comments that contain severe rule-breaking material. Just be more careful when posting from now on.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Staff only delete posts when they contain flagrantly rule breaking content.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
That was a while ago… It was pretty dumb, and it is taking up space. So can we have these 5 comments deleted?

Edited it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah, pretty much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I just wanted to bring something up about this article that occurred to me recently, and why I like it so much: It's just so fucking stupid.

Stick with me:

Rant
– hide block

Everything about this anomaly is so fucking dumb. They're humanoid chickens (one of nature's dumbest animals) that puke acid (seriously?) and lay nearly-indestructible eggs (uh, what?). They were produced by some person who wanted to refute the existence of humanity, apparently? But it the journal reads almost like an edgy teenager who's going through some sort of Lovecraftian transformation (and who apparently thinks their existence "refutes" humanity; what does that even mean?!).

Like, that's what works about this for me: This threat is so stupid. It's also just so fucking dangerous. If they got out, they could end the entire world. And what would that look like? If aliens swung by, they'd find we'd been consumed by aggressive acid-barfing chickens, all of which were now either 1) locked in a cycle of starvation/egg-laying, or 2) locked in a cycle of cannibalism/egg-laying. Our entire civilization wiped out — not by an eldritch horror, not by a shadowy malevolence — no, no, what finally got us were those fucking chickens. You know, the ones that melt your face? Yeah. Those ones.

Can you imagine an end-of-the-world scenario more stupidly horrifying than that? These things are like the xenomorphs from Alien, except the xenomorphs were designed to conform to some sort of aesthetic principle. But these things… they're just so ugly, so stupid, so utterly pointless — and so utterly in line with something that Mother-Nature, blind and oblivious, would produce.

That's what I find so compelling about these things: To me, they actually look like something natural selection would create if natural selection had access to anomalous tools. Natural selection doesn't give a fuck about aesthetic principles; it cares about results. And these things? They get results. They represent one of the dumbest end-of-the-world scenario imaginable. They're terrifying, but in a way that isn't trying to be terrifying. They're acid-spewing bird-people with indestructible eggs and anomalous reproductive habits summoned by a moody edgelord who had a creepy thing for, uh, chickens. They're monsters that aren't trying to be monsters. They just want to liquidate your insides and use them to feed their babies.

They show that no matter how clever and cunning we are, the universe could always just wipe us out with a flock of stupid, angry, acid puking ape-chickens.

This is the way the world ends: Not with a bang, but a bwaaaaauk!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair with me, I just wanted to play the game, and found the wiki by accident instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of the community, yeah, but not many successful writers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, this is true. We have had a few membership booms due to cultural attention and successful derivatives. Almost none of the members from these booms are successful writers. I think tretter is the only one that I can think of.

ETA:
We have 45,698 members.
Only 1,198 (3%) are authors.
Only 1,178 have pages that aren't in the negatives.
Only 617 (1.3%) have more than a single page.
Only 436 (0.9%) have three or more pages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excuse me if I'm wrong or off-topic, but I was under the impression that quite a lot of the community came over from the popularity of Containment Breach, just as Secret Laboratory is doing now?

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: You can check different post iterations if a comment was edited, via the bottom right of the post. The "Last edited on 22 Mar 2018" text typically indicates a post was edited.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: I think this may be in the wrong place, unless he changed it afterwards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: April Fool's Day doesn't suspend IP law.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is that a still from Jimmy Neutron?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you know what, just… ugh
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fucking Christ that thing must be what keeps nightmares awake at night. I had to put my hand over the pic so I could bear to read the containment procedures.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminded me a lot of Birdo from Mario 2.
I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quality overall. I enjoyed the implied "which came first, the chicken or the egg" joke. +1

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
This is one of the best SCP's in my opinion! I put SCP-3199 in My SCP, SCP-3216.

For the record, given that your SCP article is in the negative ratings and you've replied to the first comment in this discussion thread, it comes off a bit as attention-seeking. It's also kind of rude, since you're essentially using someone else's article thread to promote your own work.

Please try to avoid doing that in the future. It reflects badly on you and is unfair to the other authors.

SCP-3199 is very creative and very [DATA EXPUNGED].

As per site rules, this is not a roleplaying site. This sort of silly comment doesn't really come off as flattering.

item.title: Disregard
item.content:encoded: Ignore this post…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
>the latter theory holds more water

You cheeky son of a

item.title: Not sure if this is right
item.content:encoded:
Is "Humans refuted" a reference to Diogenes presenting a plucked chicken to Plato?
That's the first thing I thought when the chicken looking creature and that title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also SOMEONE EXTERMINATE THESE THINGS PLEASE IF THERE WERE 25 SCPs I COULD DESTROY UTTERLY 3199'D PROBABLY BE ON THERE

Calm down. All-caps are unnecessary here and this comment comes off as rather over-the-top as a result.

Also, for the record, most of the writers on the site here have never played Containment Breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first two times seeing 3199 were (pitch-dark except my screen) midnight browsing on r/DankMemesFromSite19
I haven't jumped that hard since I was playing Containment Breach and walked right from a 106 encounter into 173's waiting arms.
Congrats for the image!
Also SOMEONE EXTERMINATE THESE THINGS PLEASE IF THERE WERE 25 SCPs I COULD DESTROY UTTERLY 3199'D PROBABLY BE ON THERE (after more direct threats like 2317, 231-7, 319, that underwater portal to the bottom of an alien ocean that's flooding the world, etc)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m sorry, this is a little too vague for me to understand. Could you elaborate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Chickenstein? The heralds of the chicken-polisis? Anyway, take my +1

Why not just incinerates the instances of SCP-3199 to very high temperature (over 2000°C) or bury in cement for traped there for a long time? It’s OK than is not the philosophy of the Foundation “destroy-destroy-destroy the anomaly”, but if the Chickenstein are a plague, with save some instances is OK and safe.

Last question: I think that I had saw the image of the article previously. Is from some game?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's with the penis. +1
